description: Give goal ownership over the lifecycle of the .go files it emits: a versioned goal-specific header, an emit manifest, orphan warnings, opt-in --prune, and a goal clean command — all scoped so only goal-generated files are ever deleted.

userStories.title: Emit versioned goal header from pipeline
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need every .go file that goal build --emit writes to carry a goal-owned generated-code header, so that downstream tooling can identify goal-generated files without relying on a bespoke Taskfile.
acceptanceCriteria: Every .go file written by `goal build --emit` begins with a first line matching `^// Code generated by goal; DO NOT EDIT\.$`.
The second line matches `^// generator: goal v.+$` and embeds the binary's build `version` value ("dev" for a plain `go build`).
For a fixed `version`, repeated emits of the same source are byte-identical (the header adds nothing non-deterministic such as commit or date).
The Taskfile `generate` and `verify-generated` steps no longer prepend a header in shell, and the committed `internal/**/*.go` files are regenerated to carry the new header.
`task check` passes, proving the drift gate stays green with the header now emitted by goal.
userStories.notes: Linchpin story: today the header is added by Taskfile shell (Taskfile.yml:96,116), not by emitFiles (cmd/goal/main.go:1353). Moving it into the emit pipeline is what makes prune/clean usable for end users. Removing the shell prepend from BOTH generate and verify-generated is required or the drift diff will see a doubled header.

userStories.title: Add goal-generated ownership matcher
userStories.description: As a goal maintainer, I need one shared predicate that reports whether a .go file was generated by goal, so that prune and clean share exactly one definition of an owned file and never delete anything else.
acceptanceCriteria: A single exported function reports whether given Go file content is goal-generated, keyed on the goal line-1 marker and independent of the version string on line 2.
Unit test: returns true for a goal header whose line 2 reads `v dev` AND for one reading an arbitrary release version (e.g. `v9.9.9`).
Unit test: returns false for `// Code generated by protoc-gen-go. DO NOT EDIT.`, for a stringer header, and for a .go file with no generated-code header.
Unit test: returns false when a goal-looking marker appears below the header block rather than as the file's first line.
The matcher's tests run under `task check`.
userStories.notes: This is the safety core reused by US-005 and US-006. Version-agnostic on purpose: matching the exact version would strand files emitted by an older goal so prune could never reclaim them.

userStories.title: Write emit manifest of generated files
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need goal build --emit to record which files it produced, so that a later build can tell which previously-generated files have become orphaned even when their source package is gone.
acceptanceCriteria: After a successful `goal build --emit`, goal writes a manifest file at a single documented path.
The manifest lists exactly the module-relative paths of the .go files emitted in that run — no additional and no missing entries.
The manifest records the generator name (`goal`) and the build `version`.
Test: emit a fixture project, then assert the manifest's file set equals the set of emitted paths and that loading the manifest round-trips that set.
userStories.notes: A live directory scan cannot see a package whose last .goal source was deleted (goal never revisits that dir); the manifest is what remembers goal used to emit there. Prefer a committed manifest so a fresh clone can still prune deleted-package orphans.

userStories.title: Warn on orphaned generated files
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need goal build --emit to warn me when a previously-generated file is no longer produced, so that a stale .go file surfaces at build time instead of silently breaking the Go build later.
acceptanceCriteria: On `goal build --emit`, any path in the prior manifest that still exists on disk, passes the US-002 goal matcher, and is absent from the current emit set is reported to stderr as `warning: orphaned generated file <path>`.
A prior-manifest path that no longer passes the goal matcher (e.g. the user hand-edited it and removed the header) is NOT warned about.
Emitting orphan warnings does not change the exit status — the build still exits 0.
Test: seed a manifest with an extra file not in the current source, run emit, and assert the warning is printed and the orphan file still exists on disk (warn, not delete).
userStories.notes: Default-safe behavior: detect and surface, never delete. Depends on US-002 (matcher) and US-003 (manifest).

userStories.title: Delete orphans under build --prune
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need an opt-in goal build --prune that deletes orphaned goal-generated files, so that stale output is removed automatically without ever touching files goal did not generate.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal build --emit --prune` deletes the orphan set that US-004 would otherwise only warn about.
Only files passing the US-002 goal matcher are deleted; a co-located orphan bearing a non-goal generator header (e.g. `// Code generated by protoc-gen-go`) is left on disk.
A hand-written .go file with no generated-code header is never deleted, even if it appears in a stale manifest entry.
After a `--prune` run the manifest reflects only the current emit set.
Test: an orphaned goal-generated file is removed, while a protoc-marked orphan and a hand-written .go in the same tree are untouched.
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004. Prune is opt-in; default remains warn.

userStories.title: Add goal clean subcommand
userStories.description: As a goal user, I need a goal clean command that removes goal-generated .go files, so that I have a one-shot escape hatch to wipe generated output without hand-deleting files or fearing loss of hand-written code.
acceptanceCriteria: A new `goal clean [path]` subcommand removes goal-generated .go files (US-002 matcher-scoped) under the target path.
`goal clean -n` performs a dry run: it lists the files it would remove and deletes nothing.
Hand-written .go files and files bearing a non-goal generator header are never removed by `goal clean`.
`goal clean` exits 0 on success.
Test: a tree containing one goal-generated .go and one hand-written .go — `goal clean` removes only the goal-generated file, and `goal clean -n` removes nothing.
userStories.notes: Distinct from the existing Taskfile `clean` target (which removes bin/_bootstrap/dist). Matcher-based tree scan so it works as an escape hatch even without a manifest. Depends on US-002.
